overall_assessment: The project is in an early stage of development with key functionalities and non-functional requirements yet to be fully implemented.

original_input.requirements_text: We need a simple student helpdesk system. Students should create support tickets with title, description and category. Staff should view all tickets, assign tickets, add notes and update status. Students should receive notifications when ticket status changes. Basic authentication is required.

functional_requirements.statement: Students can create support tickets with a title, description, and category.
acceptance_criteria: Students can input a title, description, and select a category when creating a ticket.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can view all support tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can access a list of all submitted tickets.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can assign support tickets to themselves or others.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can select a ticket and assign it to a staff member.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can add notes to tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can append notes to the existing ticket information.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can update the status of support tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can change the status of a ticket from open to resolved and vice versa.

functional_requirements.statement: Students receive notifications when the status of their ticket changes.
acceptance_criteria: Students receive an email or alert when there is a status update on their ticket.

functional_requirements.statement: Basic authentication is required for both students and staff.
acceptance_criteria: Users must log in with a username and password to access the system.

non_functional_requirements.statement: The system shall be user-friendly and easy to navigate.
The system shall handle up to 1000 concurrent users without performance degradation.

assumptions: Students and staff have access to internet-enabled devices.
The system will be accessible via a web application.
clarification_questions: What specific categories should be available for support tickets?
What type of notifications should be implemented (e.g., email, SMS, in-app alerts)?

glossary.definition: A record detailing the request for support submitted by a student.
Process of verifying the identity of a user.

files.purpose: Project overview, setup instructions, and usage guide
files.content:
# Student Helpdesk System

## Overview
This is a Flask-based application for managing student support tickets.

## Setup Instructions
1. Clone the repository.
2. Install dependencies using `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
3. Run the application using `python app.py`.

## Usage
Access the application at `http://localhost:5000`.

files.purpose: Application entry point with Flask app initialization
files.content:
from flask import Flask
from routes import main_routes

app = Flask(__name__)

# Register routes
app.register_blueprint(main_routes)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

db = SQLAlchemy()

class Ticket(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)  # FR-01
    description = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)  # FR-01
    category = db.Column(db.String(50), nullable=False)  # FR-01
    status = db.Column(db.String(20), default='open')  # FR-05
    notes = db.Column(db.Text)  # FR-04

validation_output.summary: The generated artifacts partially meet the requirements, with several key functionalities not yet implemented and some non-functional requirements lacking evidence.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Ticket creation route defined but logic not implemented.
app/models/ticket.py - Ticket model includes title, description, and category.
issues: Ticket creation logic needs to be implemented.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Ticket retrieval route defined but logic not implemented.
issues: Ticket retrieval logic needs to be implemented.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Ticket assignment route defined but logic not implemented.
issues: Ticket assignment logic needs to be implemented.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Adding notes to ticket route defined but logic not implemented.
issues: Adding notes to tickets logic needs to be implemented.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Status update route defined but logic not implemented.
issues: Status update logic needs to be implemented.
Notification system needs to be implemented.
User authentication needs to be implemented.
Usability improvements need to be considered.
Performance testing needs to be conducted.

traceability_issues: FR-06 and FR-07 have no corresponding artifacts.
NFR-01 and NFR-02 have no corresponding artifacts.
code_quality_issues: Missing error handling in ticket routes.
No user authentication implemented, posing a security risk.
Lack of documentation for routes and models.
recommendations: Implement the logic for ticket creation, retrieval, assignment, note addition, and status updates.
Develop a notification system for ticket status changes.
Implement user authentication to secure the application.
Conduct usability testing and performance assessments.
validation_output.overall_assessment: The project is in an early stage of development with key functionalities and non-functional requirements yet to be fully implemented.

deployment_output.dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY . /app
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
EXPOSE 5000
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
